title: teams — compliance evidence
description: Teams is child-safety-critical (rosters commonly contain minors) + anti-discrimination-sensitive (trial rejections must be defensible) + financial-record-adjacent (trial fees + Wave 4 team-related invoicing). SOC 2 CC6.3, GDPR minimisation on trialist PII, WCAG 2.2 AA on the roster + trial-decision surfaces, anti-discrimination retention on rejected trials (3 years for defence against future claims).

controls.evidence: Anonymous trialist profile fields (trialist_first_name + last_name + phone + email + dob) captured at trial-scheduling for operational necessity (coach follow-up, safety incidents, age-eligibility verification). When the trial is REJECTED or NO_SHOW, the profile is redacted 90 days post-decision unless a live TeamMember row references the same person. When CONVERTED, the profile migrates into the resulting Athlete/User row and the trialist profile is cleared.

Trial scheduling implies a contract of assessment. Team roster membership implies a contract of participation. No separate consent flag required for team-membership PII beyond the ordinary tenant-signup consent.
When team.min_age < 18 OR trialist_dob < 18 years ago, roster + trial writes require verified guardian consent per the child-safety module (Wave 3 sports).

Members see their own roster history via /api/v1/teams/{team}/members?filter[member_id]=self. Trialists (registered users) see their own trials via /api/v1/teams/{team}/trials?filter[trialist_id]=self.

TenantErased cascades hard-delete of every teams / team_members / team_trials / event_teams row via FK CASCADE. Audit trail rows survive per financial + anti-discrimination retention. Personal-user erasure (member requests deletion) redacts their personal fields in place but preserves the roster row's structural fact (team X had N members in period Y) for statistical + compliance purposes — same pattern as the User module's redaction.

Trial rejection reasons live ONLY in decision_notes on the row (audited) — never propagated to analytics, activity feed, or broadcast payloads. Reason: exposure aggregates discriminatory-inference risk. The team member's `notes` field is staff-visible only; never surfaced in wire projections to the member themselves.

team-members.change-role + team-members.mark-graduated + trial.convert + trial.reject are seeded to coach + branch_manager + admin + owner only. Reception cannot approve trials or change roles. Platform.teams.force-close is ops-only + emits a notification to the tenant admin (segregation).
All /api/v1/teams routes are TLS-only + require auth:sanctum + tenant.resolve + entitlement.enforce:teams. Platform routes additionally require auth:platform_admin.
TeamMemberInvariantViolation event is a P1 signal + tracked via academorix.teams.member_invariant.violations counter (should stay at 0). The reconciler runs nightly.

The atomic trial-to-member conversion inside a single DB transaction is the mitigation for the partial-commit risk. The nightly reconciler catches drift. TeamCoachChanged fires a critical-severity audit + notification when the team includes minors, mitigating unauthorised-coach-swap risk.

TeamTrial rows with status='rejected' are retained 3 years (per retention.json). decision_notes captures the coach's rationale. Analytics NEVER carry rejection reasons. In the event of a discrimination claim, the audit trail + decision_notes are the defence record.
AgeEligibilityGate + GenderEligibilityGate refuse loudly with clear error codes (TEAM_AGE_INELIGIBLE 422, TEAM_GENDER_INELIGIBLE 422). Members see the reason in the response. No silent filtering.

controls.principle: adults working with minors must be tracked
controls.evidence: TeamCoachChanged fires a critical-severity audit row + notification to team.branch's admin + safeguarding lead when team.min_age < 18 OR team.max_age < 18. Notification cannot be opted out. Audit retention 7 years.

Team.gender_category='mixed' + min_age < 18 requires an explicit tenant configuration (settings.teams.allow_mixed_youth) — off by default. Attempted mixed-youth writes without the setting fail with a 402.

Roster grid + trial-pipeline board + member-role chip use HeroUI's design tokens which meet AA contrast by default. Status chips (active / paused / injured / suspended / archived) do not rely on colour alone — each carries a text label + icon.

controls.principle: color is not the only visual means
controls.evidence: Team.primary_color / secondary_color (jersey colours) are decorative only. Team identity in every UI surface uses name + branch + coach text, not jersey colour.
Roster + trial forms use HeroUI ComboBox + Table semantics — fully keyboard-navigable.
Trial-reject action opens a confirmation modal with the decision_notes field before commit. Team-archive requires two-step confirm when active members exist.
Trial-convert response uses aria-live=polite. Capacity-exceeded refusals return an inline error at the roster-add form.
